info.name: Partner Center REST (unofficial, generated)
info.description: Generated from partner-center-mcp. Set {{token}} to a bearer access token.

request.description:
App+User only. Returns CustomerLicensesUsageInsights (workloadName, productName, licensesActive, licensesQualified, usagePercent).
Superseded by the workload-level GET /v1/analytics/commercial/usage/license/, but this per-customer endpoint remains documented and valid.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-customer-licenses-usage-information

Note the /partner/v1/ path prefix (not /v1/). App+User only; CSP partners only.
OData params: top (max 10000), skip, filter, orderby, groupby, aggregationLevel (day|week|month).
Response includes a next link when more rows exist than 'top'.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-all-azure-usage-analytics

item.name: Get a record of Partner Center activity

request.description:
Activity log data is limited to the previous 90 days; a startDate older than 90 days returns 400.
Without startDate, results default to the previous 30 days. Dates use yyyy-mm-dd.
filter must be url-encoded JSON with Field/Value/Operator (fields: CompanyName substring, CustomerId equals, ResourceType equals); matching is case-insensitive.
Results are paged.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-a-record-of-partner-center-activity-by-user

Both country and targetView are required.
targetView is an enum (Azure, AzureReservations, MicrosoftAzure, OnlineServices, Software, MarketplaceSaaS); use OnlineServices for new commerce (NCE) license-based products.
For Azure Reservations add reservationScope=AzurePlan.
403/400036 if the targetView is not allowed; 403/400030 if the targetSegment is not allowed.
Supports both app-only and app+user.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-a-list-of-products

item.name: Get a list of availabilities for a SKU

request.description:
country is required; all segments except nonprofit are returned by default (pass targetSegment=nonprofit explicitly for those).
The availability's CatalogItemId is mutable and reissued regularly - always fetch a fresh availability right before adding a cart line item or placing an order.
For Azure Reservation SKUs add reservationScope=AzurePlan; add IncludeLifeCycleState=true for NCE license-based lifecycle state.
Supports both app-only and app+user.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-a-list-of-availabilities-for-a-sku

country and segment are required; segment currently supports only 'commercial'.
Returns only currently-active promotions (by start/end dates). Covers NCE license-based promotions.
Supports both app-only and app+user.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-promotions

country is required; promotion-id is a delimited product:sku:availability string - URL-encode it.
Returns the promotion ignoring start/end dates (works after expiry - useful for reconciliation).
Response may include promotionConstraints for eligibility checks. Supports both app-only and app+user.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-promotion-by-id

country is required; 404 with error 400013 if not found.
A product is a grouping construct and is not itself purchasable - drill into SKUs/availabilities.
Supports both app-only and app+user.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-a-product-by-id

country is required; 404 with error 400013 (product) or 400018 (SKU) if not found.
If purchasePrerequisites contains 'InventoryCheck' you must call Check Inventory before ordering.
Supports both app-only and app+user.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-a-sku-by-id

item.name: Check inventory for catalog items before ordering

request.description:
Only needed for SKUs whose purchasePrerequisites contains 'InventoryCheck'; skip otherwise.
Required InventoryContext keys vary by SKU (declared in the SKU's inventoryVariables).
Items not found in the catalog are silently omitted from the result.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/check-inventory

Paged collection: response has totalCount, items[], and links (follow links.next for more pages).
Each item carries only companyProfile and relationshipToPartner; call get-customer-by-id for full detail.
Filter with &filter={url-encoded-json} to search by company name, domain, or reseller.
Supports both app+user and app-only.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-a-list-of-customers

header.value: GUID for idempotency; reuse the same value on retries so the customer isn't created twice
header.description: GUID for idempotency; reuse the same value on retries so the customer isn't created twice

request.description:
Returns 201 with a Customer that includes userCredentials (admin username/password) - save it, it cannot be retrieved later.
Idempotent: reuse the same MS-RequestId on retries to avoid creating duplicate customers.
Required body: BillingProfile (email, culture, language, companyName, defaultAddress) and CompanyProfile (domain).
A valid physical address is required; in the sandbox an invalid address makes the customer tenant undeletable.
organizationRegistrationNumber is mandatory for customers in certain countries/regions (e.g. Russia, Turkey, Brazil, India, UAE).
Do not pass a userCredentials password in the request; it is not accepted.
Also supports app-only.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/create-a-customer

customer-id is the customer's Microsoft (Entra) tenant GUID.
Without DAP/GDAP access the response omits CompanyProfileAddress, CompanyProfileEmail, and CustomDomain.
Supports both app+user and app-only.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-a-customer-by-id

item.name: Delete a customer from the integration sandbox

request.description:
Sandbox (TIP) only - this breaks the partner-customer relationship and purges ALL resources for that tenant.
Returns 204 No Content on success.
All Azure reservations and software purchase orders must be canceled first or the delete fails.
A customer created with an invalid sandbox address cannot be deleted.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/delete-a-customer-account-from-the-integration-sandbox

item.name: Create a user account for a customer

request.description:
App+User only - app-only is NOT supported. Requires a GDAP role (User Administrator or Directory Writers).
Returns 200 OK (not 201) with the created CustomerUser including its generated id.
userPrincipalName must use the customer's own (non-federated) domain.
passwordProfile.password must meet complexity rules; set forceChangePassword=true to require a reset on first sign-in.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/create-user-accounts-for-a-customer

item.name: Get all user accounts for a customer

request.description:
App+User only - app-only is NOT supported. Requires a GDAP role (Directory Reader, Global Reader, or User Administrator).
For a single user, use /v1/customers/{customer-id}/users/{user-id}.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-a-list-of-all-user-accounts-for-a-customer

App+User only (no app-only).
If agreementType is omitted it defaults to MicrosoftCloudAgreement; pass MicrosoftCustomerAgreement explicitly for MCA confirmations.
Agreement resource is supported only in the Microsoft public cloud (not 21Vianet or US Gov).
Returns a paged collection.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-confirmation-of-customer-agreement

item.name: Confirm customer acceptance of the MCA (create agreement)

request.description:
App+User only; public cloud only (not 21Vianet or US Gov). Get the templateId via Get agreement metadata first.
201 Created on success; userId is derived from the App+User token.
Re-creating with identical contact fields returns error 600061 - vary at least one field to create another.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/confirm-customer-consent-customer-agreement

item.name: Get a download link for the MCA template

request.description:
App+User only; public cloud + MCA only. Top-level /v1/agreementtemplates path (not under /customers).
country/language default to US/en-US; values are case-sensitive.
downloadUri (Word) and displayUri (preview) are freshly generated and EXPIRE after five minutes.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/download-customer-agreement-template

Supports both app-only and app+user (unlike update-customer-qualification, which is app+user only).
Returns an array; a customer can hold multiple (e.g. Education + StateOwnedEntity).
Values: Education, GovernmentCommunityCloud, StateOwnedEntity, Nonprofit, None; vetted types carry vettingStatus.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-customer-qualification-asynchronous

POST (not PUT); app+user only. Only Education, GovernmentCommunityCloud, StateOwnedEntity can be set.
Education requires educationSegment (K12 or HigherEducation); GCC requires a validationCode + a GDAP role.
Vetted types (Education/GCC) start in a review/vetting flow rather than immediately Approved.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/update-customer-qualification-asynchronous

item.name: Get a reseller relationship request URL to send a customer

request.description:
A GET that returns a personalized { url } invitation link to send the customer; no request body.
App+User only. The customer accepts it in the Microsoft 365 admin center.
Not available for customer tenants with B2C/CIAM features enabled.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/request-reseller-relationship

item.name: Assign a directory role to a customer user

request.description:
App+User only; requires the GDAP Privileged Role Administrator role.
role-id is the directory role's GUID (from the customer's directoryroles collection).
Returns 201 Created on success.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/set-user-roles-for-a-customer

Use the dedicated /resetpassword sub-resource - NOT the generic update-user PATCH.
App+User only; needs the GDAP User Administrator (or Privileged Authentication Administrator) role.
Not supported in US Gov - use the Azure portal there.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/reset-user-password-for-a-customer

Only within the 30-day window after deletion; after that the account is purged.
App+User only; needs GDAP User Administrator or Directory Writer.
Find the deleted user-id via the deleted (UserState=Inactive) users list.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/restore-a-user-for-a-customer

item.name: Upload devices as a new batch (Autopilot)

request.description:
Returns 202 Accepted with a Location header (/customers/{id}/batchJobStatus/{tracking-id}) - poll it for status.
App+User only. Each device must satisfy one of the accepted id field combinations.
Add devices to an EXISTING batch via update-devices instead of reusing a batchId.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/upload-a-list-of-devices-to-create-a-new-batch-for-the-specified-customer

Each batch item has a devicesLink to fetch that batch's devices.
Supports both app-only and app+user.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-the-list-of-device-batches-for-the-specified-customer

item.name: Add devices to an existing batch

request.description:
Body is a bare JSON ARRAY of Device objects (unlike create-device-batch, which wraps them).
Returns 202 Accepted with a Location header to poll. Supports both app-only and app+user.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/upload-a-list-of-devices-for-the-specified-customer

The wire path is /policies (the SDK surface is named ConfigurationPolicies).
policySettings is an enum: remove_oem_preinstalls, oobe_user_not_local_admin, skip_express_settings, skip_oem_registration, skip_eula.
Supports both app-only and app+user.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-a-list-of-a-customer-s-policies

size/offset are only honored for legacy invoices; for modern commercial purchases they are ignored.
Use the filter param (LeftFilter/RightFilter/Operator JSON, bounded by InvoiceDate) to avoid timeouts on large invoice sets.
Supports both app-only and app+user.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-a-collection-of-invoices

invoice-id is a string identifier, not a GUID.
App+User only (no app-only).
For modern commercial purchases this applies only to daily-rated usage and recons generated before 2025-05-15.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-invoice-by-id

item.name: Get a customer's Azure utilization records

request.description:
start_time and end_time are REQUIRED (UTC date-time offset). granularity (daily default), show_details (default true), and size (max 1000) are optional.
NOT supported for Azure plans (only legacy MS-AZR-0145P); for Azure plans use the invoice consumption line-item APIs.
If data isn't ready in billing yet, returns 204 with a Retry-After header.
Paged - follow the next link.
Supports both app-only and app+user.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-a-customer-s-utilization-record-for-azure

item.name: Get the partner's current account balance

request.description:
The balance is exposed via InvoiceSummary.BalanceAmount; there is no dedicated balance endpoint.
Use /v1/invoices/summaries (plural) for one summary per currency.
Supports both app-only and app+user.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-the-reseller-s-current-account-balance

item.name: Get the Azure rate card (real-time prices)

header.value: e.g. en-US; also drives region/currency defaults
header.description: e.g. en-US; also drives region/currency defaults

request.description:
currency (ISO, default USD) and region (ISO country, default US) are optional; prices vary by market.
Response can be very large (~1.5 MB) - stream/parse efficiently.
All rates are pretax (isTaxIncluded is always false).
Use /v1/ratecards/azure-shared for Azure Partner Shared Services pricing.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-prices-for-microsoft-azure

item.name: Get invoice billed reconciliation line items

header.value: from the previous response's links.next when seekOperation=Next
header.description: from the previous response's links.next when seekOperation=Next

request.description:
billing-provider: OneTime (modern), or Azure/Office (legacy). invoice-line-item-type: BillingLineItems or UsageLineItems.
OneTime pages via seekOperation=Next + the MS-ContinuationToken header (from links.next); Azure/Office use size+offset. Default/max size 2000.
This v1 API is in feature freeze; for new-commerce billed recon after 2025-04-21 use the async Graph v2 API.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-invoiceline-items

header.value: from the previous response's links.next when seekOperation=Next
header.description: from the previous response's links.next when seekOperation=Next

request.description:
Literal path /invoices/unbilled/lineitems (no invoice-id). Required query: provider, invoicelineitemtype, currencycode, period (current|previous).
Pages via size (max 2000) + seekOperation=Next with MS-ContinuationToken; allow up to 24h for unbilled data.
This v1 NCE API is deprecated effective 2026-03-15; use the async v2 GA unbilled API.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-invoice-unbilled-recon-lineitems

App+User only. Response is a binary PDF (Content-Type application/pdf) - read it as bytes, not JSON.
Path is /documents/statement (singular). For Taiwan tax receipts use .../receipts/{receipt-id}/documents/statement.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-invoice-statement

item.name: Get the Azure Partner Shared Services rate card

request.description:
The 'shared' variant for Azure Partner Shared Services; the standard one is /v1/ratecards/azure.
currency/region optional (default from the partner profile). All rates are pretax.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-prices-for-microsoft-azure-partner-shared-services

item.name: Download an invoice receipt statement (Taiwan tax receipt)

request.description:
Taiwan tax receipts only - not a general invoice download. Response is a binary PDF (read as bytes).
App+User only. Needs both an invoice id and a receipt id (from the invoice's tax-receipt collection).

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-invoice-receipt-statement

item.name: Export billed reconciliation line items (Microsoft Graph, async v2)

header.description: Bearer token, audience https://graph.microsoft.com; scope PartnerBilling.Read.All

request.description:
Microsoft Graph v2; replaces the older v1 billed-recon endpoint. Returns 202 + a Location operation URL - poll it (honor Retry-After), then download the manifest's Azure Blob files.
CSP partners only; PartnerBilling.Read.All. Global cloud only (not US Gov / China).

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/graph/api/partners-billing-billedreconciliation-export?view=graph-rest-1.0

item.name: Export unbilled reconciliation line items (Microsoft Graph, async v2)

header.description: Bearer token, audience https://graph.microsoft.com; scope PartnerBilling.Read.All

request.description:
Microsoft Graph v2; both currencyCode and billingPeriod (current|last) are required. Returns 202 + Location - poll then download from the manifest.
CSP partners only; PartnerBilling.Read.All. Daily-rated usage takes ~24h to appear. Global cloud only.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/graph/api/partners-billing-unbilledreconciliation-export?view=graph-rest-1.0

Returns 201 with the LicenseUpdate resource on success.
Fails if the license is not available in the customer tenant.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/assign-licenses-to-a-user

Carts expire 7 days after creation. Success returns 201 with the populated Cart.
lineItems is required (>=1 CartLineItem). Line items are processed in array order - place any promotion-eligible (New-To-Offer) item FIRST.
billingCycle uses snake_case JSON enum values (one_time, monthly, none).
Azure plan and perpetual software line items do NOT support termDuration - omit it for those.
Set AttestationAccepted per line item when the offer enforces attestation.
Supports both app-only and app+user.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/create-a-cart

Empty request body (Content-Length: 0). Success returns 201 with a CartCheckoutResult containing an orders array.
Idempotent: re-checking out an already-checked-out cart returns the original result.
Provisioning is asynchronous - subscriptionId values may be absent in the response and appear later via Get Order.
Checkout fails on an expired cart (7-day limit).
Supports both app-only and app+user.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/checkout-a-cart

Use this (or list-customer-orders) to retrieve subscriptionId values not yet provisioned at checkout time.
Supports both app-only and app+user.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-an-order-by-id

item.name: Get all of a customer's orders

request.description:
There is a delay of up to 15 minutes between order submission and appearance in this collection - don't poll immediately after checkout.
Optional ?billingType={monthly|annual|one_time|none} filters by billing cycle.
Supports both app-only and app+user.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-all-of-a-customer-s-orders

Partner Center has no 'reservation orders' endpoint; reservations are 'reservedinstance' entitlements, each carrying a referenceOrder.id and entitledArtifacts links.
Omit entitlementType to return all entitlement types; add showExpiry=true for expiry dates.
Reservation orders have billingCycle one_time; fetch the order itself via get-order-by-id with referenceOrder.id.
Supports both app-only and app+user.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-a-collection-of-entitlements

item.name: Update an order (legacy billing cycle / cancel)

request.description:
Billing-cycle change here is for LEGACY subscriptions only; for NCE use update-subscription instead.
Out of scope: trials, monthly/Azure offers, inactive or online-services license-based subscriptions.
To cancel an order, PATCH the same path with status 'cancelled' (documented for the sandbox).

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/change-the-billing-cycle

item.name: Verify a partner by MPN id

request.description:
Omit mpnId to return the signed-in partner's profile.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-partner-by-mpn-id

App+User only. billingCurrency is fixed at account creation and cannot be changed.
Returns companyName, address, primaryContact, purchaseOrderNumber, taxId, billingCurrency, billingDay.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-partner-billing-profile

PUT (full replace), not PATCH: GET it, mutate, then PUT the whole resource back.
App+User only. Modern 'G' invoices may not reflect PO-number changes made here (may need a support request).

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/update-partner-billing-profile

item.name: Get the partner legal business profile

request.description:
Path segment is 'legalbusiness' (one word).
Returns companyName, address, primaryContact, companyApproverAddress/Email, vettingStatus/vettingSubStatus.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-legal-business-profile

App+User only. GET path is /v1/profiles/support but the UPDATE path is /v1/profiles/supportprofile - they differ.
Returns email, telephone, website.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-support-profile

App+User only. Update via PUT /v1/profiles/organization (full resource).
Returns id, companyName, defaultAddress, tenantId, domain, email, language, culture.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-an-organization-profile

header.description: Bearer token, audience https://api.partner.microsoft.com; user needs Referral Admin/User role

request.description:
Host is api.partner.microsoft.com (the Partner API), NOT api.partnercenter.microsoft.com. App+User only.
Supports OData $select/$filter/$orderby/$top; page via @odata.nextLink.
Unresponded incoming leads are archived as Expired after 14 days.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-a-list-of-referrals

item.name: Create a referral / co-sell deal (Partner API)

request.description:
Host is api.partner.microsoft.com. App+User only.
Co-sell (Shared) is a two-step flow: POST the partner-led deal, then POST a Microsoft referral linked via the returned engagementId.
For Services co-sell, PartnerRole/SolutionArea/SolutionPlay are required under details.requirements.additionalRequirements.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/create-a-referral

item.name: Get per-request MFA compliance for App+User API calls

request.description:
App+User only. Returns the most recent App+User API requests, capped at 10K items.
Each item exposes mfaCompliant (true/false) plus upn, objectId, applicationId, operationName - use it to find non-MFA-compliant callers before the MFA enforcement date.
This is request-level telemetry, not a static per-user enrollment record.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-api-request-details

item.name: List GDAP delegated admin relationships (Microsoft Graph)

header.description: Bearer token, audience https://graph.microsoft.com (NOT partnercenter); scope DelegatedAdminRelationship.Read.All

request.description:
This is a Microsoft Graph API, NOT Partner Center: host graph.microsoft.com, path starts with /v1.0/. A Partner Center token will not work.
Needs a Graph token with scope DelegatedAdminRelationship.Read.All (or ReadWrite.All); caller is in the partner tenant.
Supports OData $select/$filter/$top (max 300)/$orderby; paged via @odata.nextLink.
Not available in China (21Vianet); available in Global and US Gov.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/graph/api/tenantrelationship-list-delegatedadminrelationships?view=graph-rest-1.0

header.description: Bearer token, audience https://graph.microsoft.com; scope DelegatedAdminRelationship.ReadWrite.All

request.description:
Graph API (graph.microsoft.com), not Partner Center. Returns 201 with a Location header.
Creating it is step 1: then POST a relationshipRequest (lockForApproval), have the customer approve, then create accessAssignments.
Not available in China (21Vianet).

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/graph/api/tenantrelationship-post-delegatedadminrelationships?view=graph-rest-1.0

item.name: Get a GDAP relationship by ID (Microsoft Graph)

header.description: Bearer token, audience https://graph.microsoft.com; scope DelegatedAdminRelationship.Read.All

request.description:
Graph API. The id is a compound {partnerTenantGuid}-{customerTenantGuid}.
Response carries an @odata.etag. Not available in China (21Vianet).

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/graph/api/delegatedadminrelationship-get?view=graph-rest-1.0

header.value: set true for the new model (EventType, PageSize, PageNumber + richer alert types)
header.description: set true for the new model (EventType, PageSize, PageNumber + richer alert types)

request.description:
All query params optional. Base model: EventStatus, SubscriptionId.
With X-NewEventsModel: true you also get EventType, PageSize, PageNumber (both must be nonzero to page).
Microsoft is steering partners to the Graph partner security alerts API; this remains usable during transition.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-fraud-events

Supports both app-only and app+user.
customer-tenant-id is the customer's Microsoft (Entra) tenant GUID.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-all-of-a-customer-s-subscriptions

Add-on subscriptions include parentSubscriptionId.
NCE subscriptions return extra fields (productType, termDuration, billingCycle, refundOptions, cancellationAllowedUntilDate).
Supports both app-only and app+user.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-a-subscription-by-id

Send the FULL Subscription resource, not a partial patch: GET it first, mutate it, then PATCH it back.
If autoRenewEnabled or next-term instructions are set, include them in the body or they are silently reset.
Change quantity via Quantity; suspend/reactivate via Status (active/suspended); toggle renewal via AutoRenewEnabled.
Cancel an NCE subscription within its cancellation window by PATCHing Status to 'deleted' (see cancellationAllowedUntilDate).
Schedule changes for the next term (term/quantity/billing at renewal) via scheduledNextTermInstructions.
NCE license quantity can only be reduced within 7 days of purchase/renewal (else error 800090).
Billing cycle CANNOT be changed here (error 2054) - use update-order instead.
For SKU/term upgrades or trial-to-paid, use create-subscription-transition instead.
Long-running updates return 202 with a Location header to poll.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/update-a-subscription-by-id

item.name: Get a list of subscriptions by order

request.description:
Note the underscore: order_id, not orderId.
Returns a collection even when the order produced a single subscription.
Up to 15-minute delay after order submission before subscriptions appear.
Supports both app-only and app+user.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-a-list-of-subscriptions-by-order

item.name: Migrate a legacy subscription to New Commerce (NCE)

request.description:
Only currentSubscriptionId is required; termDuration, billingCycle, purchaseFullTerm, quantity, customTermEndDate are optional and let you change terms during migration.
Omitted properties carry forward the legacy subscription's values.
Base subscriptions migrate together with all active add-ons as a bundle; if any add-on is ineligible the whole bundle fails.
addOnMigrations must be a FLAT list, not nested.
Validate eligibility first with the validate-a-new-commerce-migration API.
To schedule rather than run immediately, POST to .../migrations/newcommerce/schedules.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/create-migration

item.name: Create a New Commerce transfer (billing ownership)

header.description: Bearer token, audience https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com; caller must be an Admin agent

request.description:
The TARGET (new) partner creates the transfer; the SOURCE partner later submits it. Only Admin agents can create transfers.
transferType must be 3 (NewCommerce) - this endpoint is not for legacy subscriptions.
Customer must already have a reseller relationship with the target partner.
Returns 201 with status Pending. Common errors: 900400, 900160, 20002.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/create-a-transfer

Either the source or target partner can read transfer status (Pending -> InProgress -> Complete).
Target partners cannot see line items until the transfer completes (new subscription/order IDs are issued then).

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-transfer-by-id

eligibilityType is optional (default immediate); use the returned catalogItemId as toCatalogItemId in create-subscription-transition.
Distinct from GET .../transitions, which returns transition history rather than eligible transitions.
Empty list for trials or when eligibilityType=scheduled (you can only transition into an existing subscription via an immediate transition).

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/transition-a-new-commerce-subscription

item.name: Create a subscription transition (NCE upgrade / trial-to-paid)

request.description:
Two-step: GET transitionEligibilities first to get a valid toCatalogItemId, then POST.
Used to upgrade an NCE subscription to a target SKU/term and to convert an NCE trial to paid.
transition_with_license_transfer needs Directory Writer (GDAP); provisioning is asynchronous (response has an Events array).

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/transition-a-new-commerce-subscription

item.name: Upgrade a customer to the Azure plan

request.description:
Global /v1/productupgrades collection (not subscription-scoped); the customer is identified in the body.
Returns 202 Accepted with an empty body and a Location header (productUpgrades/{id}) to poll; app+user only.
Check eligibility first via POST /v1/productUpgrades/eligibility. After upgrade, Get Subscriptions returns only the Azure plan.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/create-product-upgrade-entity

item.name: Get all service requests for a customer

request.description:
App+User only (no app-only).
For a single service request use GET /v1/servicerequests/{servicerequest-id}; that endpoint accepts an IncludeOrganizationRequests header for the Support Request Admin role.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-all-service-requests-for-a-customer

item.name: Get a service request by ID

header.value: authorizes the Support Request Admin role to read an org-owned request
header.description: authorizes the Support Request Admin role to read an org-owned request

request.description:
App+User only. There is no REST endpoint to list support topics or to create a service request (portal-only).
Pass IncludeOrganizationRequests to read a request owned by the organization.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/get-service-request-details-by-id

item.name: Update a service request (add note / close)

request.description:
App+User only. Send only the fields you want to change.
Add notes via the singular NewNote object (the Notes array is read-only).

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/update-a-service-request

item.name: Get Microsoft 365 service health overviews (Microsoft Graph)

header.description: Bearer token, audience https://graph.microsoft.com; scope ServiceHealth.Read.All

request.description:
Microsoft Graph (not Partner Center): reads the signed-in/own tenant, or a customer tenant via GDAP/app context.
Add $expand=issues for active issues per service; paged at 100, follow @odata.nextLink.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/graph/api/serviceannouncement-list-healthoverviews?view=graph-rest-1.0

item.name: List Microsoft 365 service health issues (Microsoft Graph)

header.description: Bearer token, audience https://graph.microsoft.com; scope ServiceHealth.Read.All

request.description:
Microsoft Graph (not Partner Center). Each issue carries classification, status, service, isResolved, posts.
Default page size 100; set Prefer: odata.maxpagesize and follow @odata.nextLink.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/graph/api/serviceannouncement-list-issues?view=graph-rest-1.0

header.description: Bearer token, audience https://graph.microsoft.com; scope ServiceMessage.Read.All

request.description:
Microsoft Graph (not Partner Center). Scope is ServiceMessage.Read.All (NOT ServiceHealth.Read.All).
Message center posts (IDs like MCxxxxxx); body content is HTML. Paged at 100, follow @odata.nextLink.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/graph/api/serviceannouncement-list-messages?view=graph-rest-1.0

Prevalidate addresses before create-customer or profile updates; the caller picks the final address from the result.
Country must be the two-character ISO alpha-2 code; required fields vary by country/region.
Only Verified or VerifiedShippable are safe to use directly; other statuses need user confirmation.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/validate-an-address

Use the HEAD method - there is no response body; rely on the status code.
404 Not Found means the domain IS available; 200 OK means it is already in use.
The {domain} segment is the full domain string, e.g. contoso.onmicrosoft.com.

Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/partner-center/developer/verify-domain-availability
